info.title: Event Storming Navigator API
info.description: API for Ontology-based Event Storming Canvas

post.description:
Upload a requirements document (text or PDF) to start ingestion.
Returns a session_id for SSE streaming of progress.

Note: Large file uploads are supported.
Text input is limited to 1024KB due to Starlette's FormData default limit.
For large content, please use file upload instead.

schema.title: Response Upload Document Api Ingest Upload Post

post.description:
Upload Figma node data (JSON) to start ingestion.
Parses Figma UI element structure into requirements for Event Storming extraction.

schema.title: Response Upload Figma Document Api Ingest Upload Figma Post

get.description:
SSE endpoint for streaming ingestion progress.
Client should connect after receiving session_id from /upload.

get.description:
Full rehydration payload for a hybrid session — actors + enriched tasks +
rules + glossary + review queue + bpmn_xml. Source of truth is Neo4j; the
frontend uses this on cold load instead of relying on localStorage.

schema.title: Response Get Session Snapshot Api Ingest Hybrid Session  Session Id  Snapshot Get

get.description: End-to-end readiness for BPM -> Rule mapping -> ES -> PRD generation.

schema.title: Response Get Pipeline Status Api Ingest Hybrid Session  Session Id  Pipeline Status Get

get.description:
SSE — explore (or replay) a single task's agentic retrieval.

`force=false` (default): if REALIZED_BY mappings already exist for this
task they are returned via an `AgentCacheHit` event without an LLM call.
`force=true` (passed by Inspector's 🔄 button): re-run the agent and
replace existing mappings.

After mappings are saved, post-explore arbitration runs to resolve any
new conflicts with already-accepted rules in other (process, task) pairs.
Cheap when there's nothing to arbitrate.

SSE — explore every task in a process (parallel, bounded concurrency).

`force=false`: tasks that already have REALIZED_BY mappings are skipped
via cache hits.
`force=true`: every task is re-explored and existing mappings replaced.

Per-task SSE events use the same names as `/task/.../retrieve` so the
frontend store handles them with the same code path.
Diagnostic: counts + small samples of every hybrid node label + key relationships.

schema.title: Response Debug Hybrid Api Ingest Hybrid Debug  Session Id  Get

delete.description:
Wipe every hybrid-owned node (all sessions), preserving analyzer + legacy event_storming.
Use this before starting a fresh hybrid run when analyzer DB shares the default Neo4j DB.
Also wipes hybrid-tagged Phase 5 promotion nodes (UserStory/Event/BC/... with session_id).

schema.title: Response Reset Hybrid Workspace Api Ingest Hybrid Reset Delete

post.description:
Start Phase 5 Event Storming promotion for a finished BPM session.

Reuses the standard ingestion infrastructure:
  1. Create an IngestionSession with source_type="hybrid" + hybrid_source_session_id
  2. Frontend subscribes to GET /api/ingest/stream/{ingestion_session_id} (existing route)
  3. The standard ingestion workflow runs, but `user_stories` phase now branches into
     hybrid mode (build_grouped_unit_contexts_from_bpm + extract_user_stories_from_bpm_group)
     and downstream phases (Event/BC/Aggregate/Command/ReadModel/Policy/...) reuse the
     existing event_storming code unchanged.
  4. After the workflow finishes, hybrid_post_workflow_hook attaches BpmTask→US/Event/Cmd
     PROMOTED_TO bridges + cross-BC Policy auto-detection.

schema.title: Response Promote Start Api Ingest Hybrid  Session Id  Promote To Es Post

delete.description: Wipe Phase 5 promotion artifacts (UserStory/Event/BC/... with this session_id).

schema.title: Response Reset Promotion Api Ingest Hybrid  Session Id  Promote To Es Delete

post.description:
Regenerate wireframe template for a UI node from an uploaded screenshot.
Uses a vision-capable LLM to interpret the image and produce wireframe HTML.

Image flow: client sends binary via multipart/form-data; server converts to
base64 only when calling the vision LLM (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google expect
data URL). Upload size is capped to avoid vision context/token limits.

schema.title: Response Ui Wireframe From Image Api Chat Ui Wireframe From Image Post

the full PRD zip.

Response Apply User Story Api User Story Apply Post

patch.description:
Partial update for a UserStory — used by the InspectorPanel save action.

Validation:
  - At least one mutable field must be present (else 400).
  - ``priority`` must be in {low, medium, high} when present (else 400).
  - ``status`` and string fields must be non-empty when present.
  - ``acceptance_criteria`` is capped at 100 entries; empty-after-trim
    entries are stripped before write.

When ``acceptance_criteria`` is present (including empty list), the
UserStory's ``criteriaUserEdited`` flag is set to true and
``criteriaEditedAt`` is set to ``datetime()`` — preventing future
ingestion regen from overwriting curated criteria (spec 019, FR-012).

schema.title: Response Patch User Story Api User Story  User Story Id  Patch

get.description: Health check endpoint.
Return the BC's `core | supporting` classification, or null when unset.

schema.title: Response Get Bc Classification Api Contexts  Bc Id  Classification Get

patch.description:
Set the BC's classification to "core" or "supporting".

Optional ``If-Match`` header carries the BC's last-known integer
version (when available); a mismatch returns 412. Version tracking
on BoundedContext is best-effort for v1 — see research R8 — so a
missing/mismatched header falls back to the unconditional write.

schema.title: Response Patch Bc Classification Api Contexts  Bc Id  Classification Patch

delete.description:
DELETE /api/graph/clear - 도메인 노드와 관계 삭제
새로운 인제스션 전에 기존 데이터를 모두 삭제합니다.

Preserved labels (NOT touched here): `:FigmaBinding`, `:StoryboardPageMapping`,
`:BindingHistoryEvent`, `:FigmaComponent` — these are user-level configuration
for spec 016 (Figma document binding) and spec 024 (scanned component catalog).
Wiping them on every re-ingestion would silently disconnect the bound Figma
document and contradict FR-005 ("Disconnection MUST NOT delete previously
generated Figma frames"); it would also defeat FR-019b's bulk-with-binding
flow because the binding row would be gone before the ingestion phase reads
it. The scanned `:FigmaComponent` catalog (spec 024) is preserved so users
don't have to re-scan after every `/clear`. Disconnect goes through the
dedicated `DELETE /api/figma-binding` endpoint, and catalog clear through
`DELETE /api/figma-binding/components` — not this one.

get.description:
GET /api/graph/stats - 그래프 통계 조회
현재 Neo4j에 저장된 노드 수를 반환합니다.

GET /api/graph/subgraph - 선택 노드 기준 서브그래프
Returns nodes and relations for the selected node IDs.

Input: Node IDs
Output: Nodes (Type, Name, Meta) + Relations (Type, Direction)

post.description:
Export a UI node's sceneGraph as a .fig file (with component assets).
Proxies to the open-pencil wireframe service.

Generate a wireframe using the .fig component library.
1. Fetch UI node context (name, description, attached command/readmodel)
2. Fetch component catalog from wireframe service
3. Call LLM to select components
4. Render via wireframe service → sceneGraph
5. Save sceneGraph to the UI node

schema.title: Response Generate Component Wireframe Api Graph Generate Component Wireframe  Node Id  Post

get.description:
Expand a node to get its connected nodes based on type.
- BoundedContext → All Aggregates + Policies
- Aggregate → All Commands + Events
- Command → Events it emits
- Event → Policies it triggers
- Policy → Commands it invokes

schema.title: Response Expand Node Api Graph Expand  Node Id  Get

get.description:
Get the BoundedContext that contains a given node.
Returns BC info so nodes can be properly grouped.

schema.title: Response Get Node Context Api Graph Node Context  Node Id  Get

Expand a node and include its parent BoundedContext.
This ensures nodes are always displayed within their BC container.

get.description:
Get all Policies triggered by an Event, along with their parent BCs and ALL related nodes.
Used when double-clicking an Event on canvas to expand triggered policies.
This fetches complete BCs with all their Aggregates, Commands, Events, and Policies.

schema.title: Response Get Event Triggers Api Graph Event Triggers  Event Id  Get

get.description:
Find ALL relations between nodes that are currently on the canvas.
This includes:
- Direct relations (HAS_COMMAND, EMITS, etc.)
- Cross-BC relations (Event TRIGGERS Policy, Policy INVOKES Command)

parameters.name: node_ids

schema.description: List of node IDs on canvas
schema.title: Node Ids

parameters.description: List of node IDs on canvas

schema.title: Response Find Relations Api Graph Find Relations Get

get.description:
Find cross-BC relationships between newly added nodes and existing canvas nodes.

This is optimized for the use case where user drops a new BC onto canvas
and we need to find connections like:
- Event (existing) → TRIGGERS → Policy (new)
- Event (new) → TRIGGERS → Policy (existing)
- Policy (existing) → INVOKES → Command (new)
- Policy (new) → INVOKES → Command (existing)

schema.title: Response Find Cross Bc Relations Api Graph Find Cross Bc Relations Get

post.description:

Command GWT bundles into the shared editor component.

schema.title: Response Get Gwt Api Graph Gwt  Parent Type   Parent Id  Get

post.description:
Parse a natural-language scenario sentence into Given/When/Then field values.

type, enum items) for each section so the LLM can map free text onto the exact

Body: {
  "type": "event" | "command" | "readmodel" | "ui",
  "name": "NodeName",
  "displayName": "표시 이름",
  "bcId": "bounded-context-id",
  "sequence": 3,
  "actor": "User",
  "aggregateId": "agg-id"  (command 전용),
  "attachedToId": "cmd-or-rm-id"  (ui 전용),
  "attachedToType": "Command" | "ReadModel"  (ui 전용),
  "isOutput": false  (ui 전용)
}

post.description:
POST /api/graph/event-modeling/relations

두 노드 간 관계 생성.
Body: {
  "sourceId": "...",
  "targetId": "...",
  "sourceType": "command" | "event" | "readmodel" | "ui" | "policy",
  "targetType": "command" | "event" | "readmodel" | "ui" | "policy"
}

sourceType/targetType 조합에 따라 적절한 관계를 자동 생성:
  Command → Event : EMITS
  UI → Command : ATTACHED_TO
  UI → ReadModel : ATTACHED_TO
  Event → Policy : TRIGGERS
  Policy → Command : INVOKES
  Event → ReadModel : CQRSConfig + CQRSOperation(TRIGGERED_BY) 자동 생성

schema.title: Response Create Relation Api Graph Event Modeling Relations Post

get.description:
Return analyzer Rules a UserStory was sourced from.

Hybrid-mode US nodes carry `(us)-[:SOURCED_FROM]->(Rule)` edges installed
by Phase 5 promote-to-es (BpmTask → REALIZED_BY → shadow Rule, fanned out
to every US sharing sourceUnitId). rfp/figma US nodes have no SOURCED_FROM
so the response `rules` list is simply empty — caller decides whether to
render the section.

get.description:
List directories under the given path for the folder picker UI.
Returns the resolved absolute path and its subdirectories.
List one level of children at `root + path` for the IDE workspace tree.
Read a single file's content + metadata for the IDE workspace editor.

put.description: Save a file's contents with optimistic-concurrency check via mtime_ns.

delete.description:
Delete a file or directory inside the workspace.

Refuses to delete the workspace root (path must be non-empty).
Directory deletion is recursive (``shutil.rmtree``).

post.description:
Move or rename a file/directory inside the workspace.

Both ``from_path`` and ``to_path`` are sandbox-checked. Refuses to
overwrite an existing destination (409) or move a directory into
itself (400).

Generate PRD files and extract them to the specified project directory.
Returns the resolved absolute path.

Uses the shared :func:`api.features.prd_generation.routes.prd_export.build_prd_zip`
helper so the on-disk artifact set produced here is **bit-identical**
to the one ``/api/prd/download`` ships — no per-BC agent files, no
``Frontend-PRD.md``, no ``.scene.json`` sidecars, role-based agents
+ ``/generate-frontend`` command emitted when applicable, and the
PRD↔CLAUDE / PRD↔.cursorrules disjointness lint enforced (hard
abort on violation).

Scan the bound Figma file's component library.

Synchronous. Returns counts (scanned/added/updated/removed) plus the
final component count for the singleton binding. Per FR-010, returns
404 if no binding, 409 if a scan is already in flight.

get.description:
Server-Sent Events stream of the active (or most recent) scan's progress.

Emits an initial `snapshot` with the current state, then one `progress`
event per state bump (phase change, VLM completion, upsert). Terminal
events `done` or `error` close the stream. Heartbeats every ~15s keep
proxies from buffering. Multiple subscribers are supported — each gets
woken on the shared progress Event.

post.description:
End-to-end smoke for spec 024 figma-with-components mode.

Flow: run the open-pencil JSX agent with the bound component catalog
injected as extra_context → post-process the returned sceneGraph
(retype `$INSTANCE:Name` FRAME leaves to INSTANCE leaves with
componentId) → push the sceneGraph to Figma via the plugin
(new page + frame). Mirrors the production figma-with-components
ingestion path (`_generate_figma_components_scene_graph`).

schema.title: Response Post Test Component Render Api Figma Binding Components  Dev Test Render Post
Response Get Storyboards Api Figma Binding Storyboards Get

get.description:
SSE variant. Emits one event per page operation as it completes,
finishing with a `done` event carrying the full summary.

post.description:
Re-run the Figma push for selected UIs (or every failed-sync UI when
uiIds is null/missing). Synchronous v1: waits for all pushes to complete
and returns the summary.

020 extensions:
  - In-flight retry deduplication via `retry_dedupe.RetryDedupeStore` —
    concurrent retries on the same uiId share one plugin dispatch.
  - Read-time classifier — non-retryable ids are skipped with a Korean
    reason. Skipped events appear in `events` as `retry_skipped`.

schema.title: Response Post Retry Sync Api Figma Binding Retry Sync Post

post.description:
RA → Figma: push the UI's current sceneGraph as an in-place update
of its linked Figma frame. Frame must already exist (UI.figmaNodeId set).

schema.title: Response Post Update Frame Api Figma Binding Update Frame  Ui Id  Post

post.description:
Figma → RA: pull the linked Figma frame's current state via the
plugin, convert to sceneGraph, persist on the UI node. No Figma API
token needed (plugin reads in-process).

schema.title: Response Post Pull Frame Api Figma Binding Pull Frame  Ui Id  Post

post.description:
Start a project-wide retroactive full-sync. Returns 202 with a runId
on success, 409 with the in-flight runId on lock contention, 404 if no
binding, 502 if binding is unreachable.

get.description:
SSE progress stream for a full-sync run. Late subscribers get the
cached run_started + most-recent progress immediately.

post.description:
Cancel a running full-sync. In-flight items finish naturally; no new
dispatches are made. Returns 404 if the run is unknown or already
terminated.

schema.title: Response Post Full Sync Cancel Api Figma Binding Full Sync  Run Id  Cancel Post

get.description:
List past :SyncRun summaries for the History tab. Each row carries a
`previousBinding` flag derived from the active binding's file key.

Canonical project-scoped failure list, classified by retryability.
Source for the History tab + ingestion floating panel + Inspector badge.

schema.title: Response Create Page Ack Api Figma Plugin Create Page Ack Post
Response Create Frame In Page Ack Api Figma Plugin Create Frame In Page Ack Post
Response Update Frame Ack Api Figma Plugin Update Frame Ack Post
Response Export Frame By Id Ack Api Figma Plugin Export Frame By Id Ack Post

get.description: Ops/debug only — frontend bootstrap does NOT call this.

schema.title: Response Get Health Api Ai Design Health Get

post.description:
Backend-driven wireframe generation for a single UI node.

Streams SSE events while the LLM agent runs (bind_tools: render, calc),
then persists the resulting sceneGraph to the UI node in Neo4j.
Body is unused for Phase 1 — the only mode is HTML (save sceneGraph).
Figma-mode push lands in Phase 2 alongside spec 016 US3.

Generate or edit a UI node's HTML wireframe template via a prompt.

Body: { instruction?: str, display_language?: "ko"|"en" }
  - instruction empty  → generate a fresh wireframe from the node's context.
  - instruction present → edit the EXISTING template per the instruction.

Reads the UI node from Neo4j, invokes the shared LLM, normalizes the HTML
fragment, persists `ui.template`, and returns the new template.

schema.title: Response Html Edit Api Ai Design Html Edit  Ui Node Id  Post

post.description:
OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoint (streaming-first).

Note: we accept the request body via `req.json()` so we can be lenient
about Pydantic schema drift between OpenAI versions. If `stream=false`
is requested we still stream — open-pencil's `@ai-sdk/vue` always uses
streaming, and rewriting non-streaming would be wasted code today.
T022: synchronous full-set generation for one Bounded Context.
T030: refresh just one Aggregate Spec file.
T027: (re)generate ``specs/context-map.md``.
T033: SSE-streamed full-model bootstrap.

get.description: Epic(BoundedContext) → Feature → UserStory → AcceptanceCriteria tree.

post.description: Create an Epic (BoundedContext). MERGEs on the name-derived key.

patch.description: Rename / re-describe an Epic (BoundedContext). Relationships preserved.

get.description: 로컬 Claude IDE + speckit 설치 상태 점검 (US5 claude-ide 엔진 사전 점검).

post.description:
Propose child User Stories for an Epic ('epic') or Feature ('feature').

`engine`: 'in-process' (backend LLM) or 'claude-ide' (local claude CLI,
falls back to in-process on failure).
Persist the user-selected generated stories under their BC (+Feature).
요구사항의 DDD 적합성·입도·정합성을 검증하고 교정안을 제안(비차단).
자연어 설명에서 Epic(BoundedContext) 후보를 제안한다(미확정).
자연어 설명에서 Feature 후보를 제안한다(미확정). 소속 Epic 컨텍스트 반영.

get.description: 설계가 아직 반영되지 않은 User Story 목록을 반환한다(범위 옵션).

post.description: 선택된 미반영 User Story들에 대해 Aggregate→Command→Event 설계를 생성·반영한다.
Create a new Feature under a Bounded Context (source='manual').

delete.description: Delete a Feature. Child user stories are unassigned or deleted.

patch.description: Rename / re-describe a Feature (034). Child user stories stay attached.

post.description: Decompose natural-language text into proposed user stories. No mutation.
Persist a user story (from a proposal or manual input).

patch.description: Re-assign a user story to a different Feature (drag-n-drop).

get.description: Load the policy→command→event design trace for a User Story.
Fetch an impact report by id (poll until status is done/failed).
Stream an impact report via SSE until it reaches a terminal state.

post.description:
Start a clarification session for a scope. Returns immediately with
`status=analyzing`; the deep-agent scan runs as a background task and
streams progress via `/stream`.

get.description: Replay-then-tail the session progress event buffer as SSE.
Snapshot of a session — SSE reconnect fallback (FR-013).

post.description:
Submit an answer; returns an encoded edit proposal — graph not yet
mutated. `mode=skip` returns an empty proposal and advances the queue.

Apply the encoded edits for a question. Re-uses the user-story edit
path so optimistic locking, no-op detection, and impact analysis are
inherited.
Finalize a session (applied answers retained, unanswered untouched).

get.description: Return the session summary — `/end` may or may not have been called.

post.description: Restore a single requirement to its pre-session snapshot.

get.description: Aggregate `UserStory.clarifications` across the scope, chronologically.

post.description: Discard an in-progress session — applied answers stay in the graph.

get.description:
Snapshot of in-memory pending-clarification flags per UserStory.

Returned shape: `{ "userStoryFlags": { "<usId>": FlagInfo, ... } }`.
The frontend hits this when it loads the requirements tree so it can
render an ambiguity badge on flagged user stories.

schema.title: Response Get Clarification Flags Api Requirements Clarification Flags Get

get.description:
Per-category clarity score for a scope — drives the radar chart.

Returns 10 axes (one per `AmbiguityCategory`), each scoring [0,1]
where 1.0 means no in-scope requirement is currently flagged for
that category. Together with `flaggedUserStories` and `totalUserStories`
the frontend can render a polygon and an overall % gauge.
Liveness probe for the robo-spec sub-system.

schema.title: Response Health Api Robo Spec Health Get

display_language.title: Display Language
display_language.description: Display language for wireframe text (ko or en)

file.title: File
file.description: 단일 업로드 (기존 호환). `files`와 함께내면 모두 합쳐 처리합니다.

files.title: Files
files.description: 같은 필드명 `files`로 여러 파일을 보낼 수 있습니다 (multipart).

score.title: Score
score.description: 1.0 fully clear · 0.0 every requirement flagged. Weights: clear/resolved=1.0, deferred=0.5, outstanding=0.0

CategoryClarityScore.title: CategoryClarityScore
CategoryClarityScore.description:
Clarity score for one ambiguity category, used by the radar chart.

Mirrors the SpecKit `/speckit-clarify` skill's step-8 coverage model:
`status` is one of {clear, resolved, deferred, outstanding} when an
agent scan has reported on this category for the scope; `None` when
no scan has run yet (the score then comes from the flag/log fallback).

ChangePlanRequest.title: ChangePlanRequest
ChangePlanRequest.description: Request for generating or revising a change plan.

overallScore.title: Overallscore
overallScore.description: Average of per-category scores — convenience for the dashboard header.

DraftChange.title: DraftChange
DraftChange.description:
A proposed change emitted by the LLM during /api/chat/modify (draft-only).

- For update actions, `updates` is the field-patch map (source of truth).
- For compatibility with the existing canvas sync, we may also include some
  flattened fields (e.g., description/template) in applied responses, but drafts
  are primarily driven by `updates`.

expected_mtime_ns.title: Expected Mtime Ns
expected_mtime_ns.description: Required for existing files; None creates a new file.

FrontendFramework.title: FrontendFramework
FrontendFramework.description:
Frontend frameworks supported by the PRD-generation flow.

v1 ships ``vue`` and ``react``; the 2026-05-12 amendment (feature 022 P5)
adds ``svelte``. Adding a new framework requires registering its
conventions in
:data:`api.features.prd_generation.prd_tech_stack_catalog.FRAMEWORK_CONVENTIONS`
so ``specs/frontend/framework.md`` can render its conventions block.

GenerationWarning.title: GenerationWarning
GenerationWarning.description: A non-fatal warning surfaced during requirement decomposition.

LocalToolingStatus.title: LocalToolingStatus
LocalToolingStatus.description: 로컬 Claude IDE + speckit 설치 상태 (034 US5 — claude-ide 엔진).

ModifyRequest.title: ModifyRequest
ModifyRequest.description: Request to modify selected nodes based on a prompt.

node_ids.title: Node Ids
node_ids.description: List of node IDs from canvas. Note: PRD generation always includes all Bounded Contexts regardless of this parameter.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Optional session scope. When provided, PRD generation only includes BCs with this session_id.

ReferenceCandidateDTO.title: ReferenceCandidateDTO
ReferenceCandidateDTO.description: A Command of the same Aggregate offered as a reference candidate.

SetupProjectRequest.title: SetupProjectRequest
SetupProjectRequest.description:
Extract generated PRD files to a target directory for Claude Code.

``output_mode`` (feature 029) controls which structure is laid down:

- ``"robo-spec"`` (default, recommended): skip the legacy PRD pipeline
  entirely; only install the verbatim robo-spec skill set + speckit
  inheritance chain + ``.mcp.json`` + ``.claude/robo-project.json``.
  The user generates ``plan.md`` / ``tasks.md`` / source on demand in
  Claude Code via ``/robo-plan``, ``/robo-tasks``, ``/robo-implement``.
- ``"prd"``: pre-029 behavior — generate the full PRD ZIP
  (PRD.md, specs/, .cursor/rules/, agents/, ...) AND ALSO install the
  robo-spec skills on top. ``prd_request.tech_stack`` is required.

SpecFormat.title: SpecFormat
SpecFormat.description:
How per-BC spec markdown is laid out inside the generated package.

- ``prd``: per-BC folder layout — ``specs/<bc_name>/index.md`` per BC,
  auto-split into per-aggregate or per-command files when the BC's
  monolithic spec exceeds ``BC_SPEC_SPLIT_LINE_THRESHOLD`` (800 lines
  by default).
- ``ddd``: the "DDD for SDD" artifact set from feature 022 —
  ``specs/bounded-contexts/<bc-slug>/{domain-terms,bc-<slug>,aggregates/aggregate-*,acl-*,requirements}.md``
  plus ``specs/context-map.md``. Generated via ``api.features.ddd_spec``.

project_name.title: Project Name
project_name.description: Project name for the generated code

ai_assistant.description: AI assistant to use: cursor or claude

spec_format.description: Per-BC spec layout: 'prd' (legacy flat) or 'ddd' (DDD-for-SDD artifact set; feature 022)

UpdateUserStoryRequest.title: UpdateUserStoryRequest
UpdateUserStoryRequest.description:
Partial-update body for ``PATCH /api/user-story/{id}``.

Every field is optional. Submitting a body with no recognised mutable
field results in HTTP 400. ``acceptance_criteria`` distinguishes
'not in payload' (None) from 'set to empty list' ([]) — only the latter
flips ``criteriaUserEdited`` to true.

VectorSearchRequest.title: VectorSearchRequest
VectorSearchRequest.description: Request for keyword-based related object search.

VectorSearchResult.title: VectorSearchResult
VectorSearchResult.description: A single result from keyword-based related object search.
